description: 5G testbed — complete view: testbed health, registration procedure, session management, data plane, policy, resource usage and logs

panels.description: Step 1 of UE attach. The UE sends a NAS Registration Request to the AMF via gNB. In a healthy attach Req and Succ track closely. Any Fail count indicates a problem in the registration procedure.
panels.title: Initial Registration — Request vs Success vs Fail

panels.description: AMF triggers authentication after receiving a Registration Request. Auth Req count should equal Reg Req count. Fails = wrong KI/OP or SIM config mismatch. Rejects = network decision to deny access.
panels.title: Authentication (5G-AKA)

panels.description: Mobility reg = UE moved between tracking areas. Periodic reg = UE signals it is still reachable on a timer. Normal in an idle attached UE.
panels.title: Mobility & Periodic Registration

panels.description: AMF pages an idle UE to deliver downlink data. High paging rate with low success indicates UE is not reachable — possible RAN or UE coverage issue.
panels.title: Paging (MM)

panels.description: After successful registration the UE requests a PDU session to obtain an IP address. SMF coordinates with UPF and PCF to establish the session.
panels.title: PDU Session Creation

panels.description: SMF programs the UPF via PFCP over N4. SMF and UPF N4 Estab counters should be equal. Reports indicate active session heartbeats and usage reporting.
panels.title: N4 Session Establishment — SMF ↔ UPF (PFCP)

panels.description: PCF creates policy associations for UE Access Management (AM) and Session Management (SM). AM Policy controls mobility behaviour. SM Policy controls session QoS and charging rules.
panels.title: Policy Associations — PCF (AM & SM)

panels.description: Actual user data packets traversing the N3 GTP tunnel. Uplink = UE sending data. Downlink = UE receiving data. Zero here means no active data traffic even if the session is established.
panels.title: N3 GTP Packet Rate — gNB ↔ UPF

panels.description: Container-level byte counters for the UPF. Includes N3 (gNB), N4 (SMF) and N6 (internet) interface traffic combined.
panels.title: UPF Container Throughput (Bytes)

panels.description: Value = number of CPU cores used. 0.5 means half a core. Calculated as (cpuDelta / systemDelta) × numCPUs.
panels.title: CPU Usage — Core NFs (cores used)

title: 5G Core — Complete
